fix: preserve latest Codex assistant turn output (#100)

* test(BUB-129): restore workspace test import boundaries

Summary:
- switch cross-package test imports to package-name entrypoints
- use plugin source subpaths for CLI plugin runtime tests to avoid dist build-order coupling

Rationale:
- merged origin/main introduced stricter workspace boundary lint rules
- CLI unit tests should not depend on plugin dist artifacts existing before the CLI workspace runs

Tests:
- npm run check && npm test

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(BUB-129): preserve latest codex assistant output text

Summary:
- let the codex adapter keep the latest assistant output_text for turn output
- add a regression test covering final_answer followed by richer response_item output_text
- record the adapter precedence pattern in progress.txt

Rationale:
- codex transcripts can emit a placeholder final_answer before the fuller assistant message
- turn output should reflect the final assistant text that normalize later exports to OTLP

Tests:
- npm run check && npm test

commit.message:
fix(BUB-133): route export-json through canonical events (#101)

* test(BUB-133): align cli unit imports with package boundaries

Summary:
- switch CLI unit tests from cross-package relative imports to package-name imports
- cover backend, OTLP, OpenClaw, and OpenCode test entry points

Rationale:
- origin/main now enforces import boundaries via no-restricted-imports
- these test-only changes restore green validation after merging main

Tests:
- npm run check && npm test && npm run test:bdd

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(BUB-133): route export-json through canonical events

Summary:
- write export-json payloads with canonical rawEvents while keeping OTLP and
  JSONL on backendEvents
- thread rawEvents through CLI export call sites and add BDD coverage for
  Codex context event preservation
- record the canonical/backend split pattern in progress.txt

Rationale:
- report and alert flows read canonical agentic.context fields from exported
  JSON and should not inherit backend-only Langfuse mutations
- OTLP and JSONL still need backend-adapted events to preserve existing
  external behavior

Tests:
- npm run check && npm test && npm run test:bdd
- cd packages/cli && npx vitest run test/unit
- node --import <mock-fetch> packages/cli/dist/index.js runtime codex export
  ... && node packages/cli/dist/index.js report context --input-json ...

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Add Mathlib naming notes and repo audit

Write two artifact notes on Mathlib naming conventions and the current naming debt in the Bridgeland stability repo.

The first note records the corrected naming rules that matter here, including the distinction between theorem-style proof names and proposition-like named objects, the role of namespaces, the treatment of embedded UpperCamelCase concepts inside proof names, and the implementation split between proof-only subtypes and richer bundled wrappers.

The second note applies those conventions to a fresh audit of the repository. It corrects the earlier overbroad casing pass, identifies the highest-priority rename targets, records the design-linked naming problem around NumericalStabilityCondition, and separates immediate theorem-name fixes from larger abbreviation-family cleanups.

No Lean code changes are included in this commit; it adds documentation artifacts only.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat: heartbeat verbosity as first-class control (#30)

Add 4 verbosity levels (conversational, standard, detailed, debug)

commit.message:
fix: heartbeat system overhaul — 10 fixes (#29)

* fix: heartbeat system overhaul — signals_only, confidence gating, tag tiers, stability weighting

Critical fix: add signals_only mode so plugin gets fresh signals after watcher
already decided to act, instead of re-running decision pipeline and hitting
cooldown. Also fixes 9 additional heartbeat issues:

- Add CheckPositiveDeltas/CheckMemoryVelocity to allChecks, gate computation
- Stale escape re-acts with real signals instead of always falling to nudge
- PendingWork recency gate: filter plans older than 7d without upcoming deadline
- Topic dedup window uses SignalCooldownNormal instead of hardcoded 1h
- Confidence gating: exclude low-confidence memories from signals (default 0.5)
- Tag-based tier adjustment: critical/urgent boost, low-priority/backlog demote
- Stability weighting in confluence scoring
- Response rate multiplier uses smooth linear interpolation instead of step function
- Remove duplicate comment block

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: exclude expired pending-work deadlines

* fix: record stale-escape acts

* fix(heartbeat): preserve signals_only signals

---------

ci: merge auto-tag into release.yml, eliminate PAT requirement (#75)

Fold tag creation into release.yml's new prepare job so the merged
chore(release): vX.Y.Z commit on main is the single release trigger.
No PAT / RELEASE_AUTOMATION_TOKEN needed — default GITHUB_TOKEN
pushes the tag since it doesn't need to trigger another workflow.

- Delete auto-tag-release.yml
- Add prepare job: validates commit, creates tag, exports metadata
- Update all downstream jobs to consume prepare outputs
- workflow_dispatch kept as retry path for existing tags
- Update scripts/release.sh and CLAUDE.md docs

Co-authored-by: codex <codex@openai.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

rendering, escaping, and edge cases.

commit.message:
ci: fast release pipeline — auto-tag, CI fast path, release script (#72)

- Add auto-tag-release.yml: detects chore(release): vX.Y.Z on main,
  validates against Cargo.toml, creates+pushes tag via PAT so release.yml
  triggers without manual handoff
- CI fast path: skip heavy jobs (clippy, MSRV, docs, cargo-deny, gitleaks,
  browser-scripts, 3-OS builds) for release-only PRs that touch only
  Cargo.toml/Cargo.lock/CHANGELOG.md. Keeps fmt + test as fast blockers.
- Remove changelog CI job (release notes generated in release.yml)
- Add scripts/release.sh: one-command release prep (bump, check, commit,
  push, PR)
- Document new flow in CLAUDE.md

Co-authored-by: codex <codex@openai.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-authored-by: codex <codex@openai.com>

Revamp onboarding flow, randomize placement test, and add Beginner Guide tip

### Motivation
- Fix a few critical onboarding UX issues: the placement test always had the same answer order, users could be bounced back into onboarding after finishing, and the onboarding UI was not responsive or clearly guiding new users.
- Make onboarding more usable for international users by switching the flow copy/UI to English and making the visual presentation more dramatic and responsive.
- Provide a clear first-session path by highlighting a `Beginner Guide` on the main page so newly onboarded users have a concrete next step.

### Description
- Rebuilt `korehan/korehan-onboarding.html` into a responsive, English-language onboarding experience with multi-select goals, 5/15/30/60 minute time options, self-select-or-test level flow, and a modern two-column layout.
- Replaced the static test implementation with a randomized placement test (`TEST_BANK` + `shuffle` + `buildTestQuestions`) that randomizes both question selection and answer order and lets users override the recommended level.
- Persist onboarding completion locally and to Supabase by adding `saveOnboardingLocal()` and `persistOnboardingState()` and updating `finishOnboarding()`/`skipOnboarding()` to upsert `user_stats` to avoid redirect loops.
- Updated `korehan/beginner-guide.html` into a responsive first-time user guide that points to `korehan-all.html?level=Beginner`, `korehan-study-room.html`, `korehan-learning-overview.html`, `korehan-conversations.html`, and `korehan-stories.html`; and changed `korehan/korehan-shared.js` to show a highlighted Beginner Guide CTA for new users (`renderHomeLearningPreview` + onboarding check improvements).

### Testing
- Ran `node --check korehan/korehan-shared.js` to validate shared JS syntax and the check passed.
- Extracted the inline onboarding script and ran `node --check` on the extracted JS to validate syntax after the refactor and the check passed.
- Parsed the updated HTML files with Python's `html.parser` to ensure well-formed HTML (checked `korehan/korehan-onboarding.html` and `korehan/beginner-guide.html`) and parsing succeeded.
- Verified the new onboarding logic by running the code-path validations in the development environment (local syntax checks and HTML parsing all succeeded).

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Merge pull request #5 from ricardotwumasi/add-agents-md

This adds the AGENTS.md to formalise AI agent contribution guidelines, for security, ethics and reproducibility.

Co-authored-by: Claude noreply@anthropic.com￼
Co-authored-by: Codex codex@openai.com￼

repository.name: dj_mentor
repository.description: Your DJ CoPilot. An intelligent DJ system that teaches beginners to mix like professionals in real time.

commit.message:
feat: add AGENTS.md to formalise AI agent contribution guidelines

This commit introduces a new AGENTS.md file, derived from the existing CLAUDE.md, to generalise and standardise guidance for all AI coding agents contributing to the DJ Mentor codebase (including Claude, Codex, and similar systems).

The original CLAUDE.md was specific in scope and framing. This new AGENTS.md expands that intent into a model-agnostic specification, ensuring consistent behaviour across multiple AI providers while preserving the same architectural, safety, and real-time performance constraints.

Key aspects of this addition:

- Generalises assistant instructions from Claude-specific to multi-agent compatibility
- Preserves strict unidirectional data flow (Zustand as single source of truth)
- Explicitly protects critical real-time audio and MIDI files from modification
- Formalises security constraints (input validation, IPC restrictions, CSP rules, secret handling)
- Encodes safe AI execution boundaries (limited autoExecute actions, confirmation for destructive controls)
- Reinforces non-blocking requirements for all AI-related operations in the audio loop
- Documents testing requirements (Vitest, TypeScript, ESLint) as mandatory pre-commit checks
- Clarifies development principles (minimal changes, no new state systems, respect existing abstractions)
- Extends commit attribution conventions to include multiple AI agents

This change is intentionally conservative in scope: it does not modify runtime code, but instead strengthens contributor alignment and reduces the risk of unsafe or architecture-breaking AI-generated changes.

The AGENTS.md should now be treated as the primary instruction surface for any automated or AI-assisted development workflows.

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

repository.name: dj_mentor
repository.description: Your DJ CoPilot. An intelligent DJ system that teaches beginners to mix like professionals in real time.

commit.message:
docs(issue-plan): refresh OPE-139 traceability for Go mainline (#153)

Summary:
- Replaced the OPE-139 minimum trace set pointers from legacy Python paths
  to Go-mainline paths under bigclaw-go.
- Kept the existing command-center, approval, saved-views, dashboard,
  and replay evidence groupings unchanged for audit continuity.

Rationale:
- BigClaw mainline execution and review now land in the Go implementation,
  so the issue-plan evidence pointers should default to Go-owned artifacts.
- This keeps planning traceability aligned with current implementation and
  test ownership after cutover.

Refine vehicle fuel-first mobile layout

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

refactor: extract load_pretrain_weights + apply_lora into models/weights.py (#840)

Eliminates duplicated weight-loading and LoRA application logic between
detr.py (_load_pretrain_weights_into, _apply_lora_to) and module_model.py
(_load_pretrain_weights, _apply_lora).  The canonical unified implementation
lives in the new src/rfdetr/models/weights.py and both callers delegate to it.

Key changes:
- New src/rfdetr/models/weights.py: load_pretrain_weights() uses the more
  complete module_model.py logic (Pydantic model_fields_set for user-override
  detection, auto-align to fine-tuned checkpoints, expand-back for explicit
  num_classes) plus class_names extraction from detr.py.  apply_lora() is
  the unified LoRA application function.
- detr.py: removes ~80 lines of private weight/LoRA methods; delegates to
  weights.py; fixes mutable COCO_CLASS_NAMES returned directly from
  class_names property (now returns list copy).
- module_model.py: removes ~95 lines of private weight/LoRA methods;
  delegates to weights.py.
- tests/models/test_weights.py: 8 new characterization tests (4 reinit
  scenarios, 2 class_names extraction, 2 LoRA application).
- test_load_pretrain_weights.py, test_module_model.py, test_detr_shim.py:
  migrated patch targets and call sites to rfdetr.models.weights.
- Pin torchvision>=0.17.0 (aligns with torch>=2.2.0 floor)
- Pin pydantic>=2.0,<3 (formalises existing v2-only API usage)
- Move peft from core deps to [lora] and [train] optional extras
- Replace top-level `from peft import PeftModel` in backbone.py with
  lazy try/except import inside Backbone.export()
- Fix merge_and_unload() result not being assigned back to self.encoder
- Add ImportError hints in _apply_lora_to() and _apply_lora() pointing
  users to `pip install rfdetr[lora]`
- Add characterization test: importing backbone must not pull peft into
  sys.modules
- Add regression test: Backbone.export() replaces encoder with merged model
- `_RemovedModuleFinder.find_spec` now short-circuits on any `fullname`
  that does not start with `rfdetr.`, preventing bare top-level imports
  such as `import util` or `import deploy` from being hijacked by the
  RF-DETR migration finder after `import rfdetr`.
- Replace the type-identity dedup guard with a `sys`-level sentinel
  (`sys._rfdetr_removed_finder`) so `importlib.reload(rfdetr)` never
  inserts a second finder into `sys.meta_path`.
- Add two characterization tests: scope guard (bare names return None)
  and reload idempotency (finder count unchanged after reload).

---------

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Phase 3B: Channel system — event routing, transports, push notifications

Event router (event_router.rs):
- Route events by EventFilter to multiple targets
- Target types: McpChannel, FileNotification, Webhook, AgentStdin
- Auto-cleanup on agent exit (remove_session)

Transports (transports.rs):
- McpChannelTransport: JSON format for Claude Code MCP channel capability
- FileTransport: JSONL append with timestamps
- WebhookTransport: async HTTP POST with timeout
- StdinTransport: human-readable notification via daemon client

Formatters (formatters.rs):
- Human-readable notifications for StatusChange/AgentExited/TaskUpdate/ContextWarning/LoopDetected
- JSON serialization for programmatic consumption

Channel binary (main.rs):
- CLI args with clap (--socket, --config)
- Connects to daemon, subscribes to all events
- Routes and pushes via configured transports
- TOML config support for default routes and webhooks

788 LOC channel, 67 tests total, clippy clean

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Make the adoption path obvious for new readers

The public README already documented the reusable bootstrap and skills, but a first-time reader still had to scan the page to figure out the fastest path to using it in their own repository.

Add a short copy-paste adoption section near the top with the bootstrap command and the minimal follow-up needed for extra co-authors.

Constraint: Keep the quick-start addition short and avoid re-explaining details already covered later in the README
Rejected: Replace the fuller reuse section | the quick block is complementary, not a substitute for the detailed notes
Confidence: high
Scope-risk: narrow
Reversibility: clean

Surface CI status directly in the public README

The repository already had a working verification workflow, but readers landing on the public page had no immediate signal that the bundle was currently passing its self-test.

Rejected: Add a separate CI section at the top | the badge is a lighter, more standard signal for repository health

commit.message:
Keep the public sample repo self-verifying

The repository had become public and reusable, but it still relied on manual local checks to prove that the attribution bundle actually worked after future edits.

Add a reusable verification script plus a GitHub Actions workflow that bootstraps the bundle into a fresh temporary Git repository and confirms a generated commit receives the Codex co-author trailer.

Constraint: The CI check must validate real Git behavior, not just file presence
Rejected: Lint-only workflow | it would miss regressions in bootstrap or commit-message injection behavior
Confidence: high
Scope-risk: narrow
Reversibility: clean
Directive: Keep CI aligned with the local verification script so repository and workflow checks do the same end-to-end validation
Tested: bash -n on the new verification script; ran the full local verification flow successfully; confirmed actions/checkout latest release is v6.0.2 before pinning the workflow to @v6
Not-tested: Execution of the new workflow inside GitHub Actions after push

Co-authored-by: codex <codex@openai.com>

repository.description: Sample Git hook + Codex skills for GitHub co-author attribution

commit.message:
Make the sample attribution setup reusable across repositories

The sample repository had become a good reference, but using it in another project still required manually copying files and remembering which pieces belonged together.

Add a bootstrap script that copies the managed hook, setup script, and repo-local Codex skills into another existing Git repository, then runs the target setup script automatically.

Constraint: Avoid maintaining duplicate template trees with the same hook and skill content
Rejected: Separate template directory with copied files | duplication would drift from the source of truth
Confidence: high
Scope-risk: narrow
Reversibility: clean
Directive: Keep the bootstrap file list explicit so changes to managed attribution assets remain reviewable
Tested: bash -n for all shell scripts; bootstrap into a fresh temporary Git repo; verified copied files, local Git config, skill paths, and a test commit with the Codex co-author trailer
Not-tested: Overwrite flow with --force against an already customized target repo

Co-authored-by: codex <codex@openai.com>

repository.name: sample-codex-coauthor
repository.description: Sample Git hook + Codex skills for GitHub co-author attribution

commit.message:
Make attribution health checks one command away

The sample repository could install and manage co-author trailers, but it still lacked a quick read-only health check for confirming that the hook, config, and recent commits were aligned.

Add a third repo-local skill that prints the local hook path, commit template, configured ai.coauthor values, and the co-author trailers on the latest commit.

Constraint: Keep the status check read-only and repository-local
Rejected: Infer status from files alone | Git config and commit history are the actual source of truth
Confidence: high
Scope-risk: narrow
Reversibility: clean
Directive: Keep this skill diagnostic-only; do not let it mutate Git config or commit history
Tested: bash -n for all shell scripts; ran the status script and verified reported hook path, template, co-authors, and latest trailer
Not-tested: Skill rendering in Codex app or IDE surfaces

commit.message:
Make additional co-author trailers easy to manage

Add a second repo-local skill that lists, adds, and removes ai.coauthor values so people can manage extra trailers without editing hook files or memorizing git config syntax.

Constraint: Do not guess provider emails that are not verified in this repo
Rejected: Hard-code Gemini and Claude presets | unsupported or unverified emails would create misleading defaults
Confidence: high
Scope-risk: narrow
Reversibility: clean
Directive: Keep provider-specific presets conservative; only bake in values that are verified and intentionally supported

Not-tested: Codex skill selection UX in every surface

commit.message:
Phase 3A: Intelligence layer — context rotation, cross-review, auto-recovery

Context monitor (context_monitor.rs):
- Estimates context usage from agent output patterns + byte proxy
- Rotation threshold (default 80%), rotation prompt with task/progress/files/decisions
- Agent-specific max tokens (Claude 200K, Codex 192K, Gemini 1M)

Cross-review engine (review_engine.rs):
- Adversarial cross-model review pairs (Claude↔Codex, Gemini→Claude)
- Structured review prompt with 5 categories (correctness/perf/security/style/arch)
- Parser for VERDICT/ERROR/WARNING/NIT/SUGGESTED FIX format
- needs_changes: Error severity or RequestChanges blocks merge

Auto-recovery (recovery.rs):
- Decision tree: loop→restart, stuck→nudge→restart→escalate, error→retry→escalate
- Tailored nudge/restart prompts with context preservation
- Configurable thresholds (idle 5min, loop 5x, max 3 retries)

Integration:
- PtyManager: health_check(), trigger_rotation(), request_review()
- socket_server: RequestReview handler returns structured ReviewRequest

875 LOC intelligence, 58 tests total, clippy clean

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Phase 2B: MCP server (12 tools, JSON-RPC stdio) + task planner

MCP server (rist-mcp):
- main.rs: JSON-RPC 2.0 stdio transport, initialize/tools_list/tools_call
- tools.rs: 12 tool definitions with JSON Schema + daemon-backed handlers
  (spawn_agent, list_agents, get_agent_output, write_to_agent, kill_agent,
   archive_agent, wait_for_idle, run_command, read_task_graph, write_task_graph,
   get_file_ownership, merge_agent)
- MCP protocol: 2024-11-05, structuredContent support

Task planner (ristd):
- planner.rs: TaskPlanner with dependency-aware scheduling
  (ready_tasks, blocked_tasks, assign_task, stats, atomic save/load)
- Integrated into daemon socket_server for ReadTaskGraph/WriteTaskGraph

Daemon client extensions:
- spawn_agent_with_options, archive_agent, wait_for_idle, run_command
- read_task_graph, write_task_graph, preview_merge, merge_agent, get_file_ownership

35 tests passing, clippy clean, 858 LOC MCP + planner

commit.message:
BIG-PAR-219: github-sync status relation metrics (#152)

* BIG-PAR-103: fix repo-root path resolution for workspace validate

* BIG-PAR-103: record tracker closeout

* BIG-PAR-103: append final tracker note

* BIG-PAR-103: fail closed on lease expiry and surface lease-loss

* BIG-PAR-103: tracker progress update

* fix(queue): harden shared-queue lease safety (BIG-PAR-103)

Summary:
- Serialize SQLite leasing with BEGIN IMMEDIATE to prevent double-lease races.
- Enforce lease ownership + attempt checks for file/memory queue operations.
- Reject requeue/dead-letter transitions once a task is cancelled/dead-lettered.
- Add a regression test covering concurrent LeaseNext across two SQLite clients.

Rationale:
- Multi-worker and multi-node runs share one SQLite queue file. Deferred
  transactions could allow multiple processes to select and lease the same
  task before either UPDATE executes.

Tests:
- cd bigclaw-go && go test ./...

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* BIG-PAR-103: purge cancelled leases after expiry

* BIG-PAR-103: tracker progress update (expiry purge)

commit.message:
Lease-safety hardening for shared queue coordination (#147)

* BIG-PAR-103: fix repo-root path resolution for workspace validate

* BIG-PAR-103: record tracker closeout

* BIG-PAR-103: append final tracker note

* BIG-PAR-103: fail closed on lease expiry and surface lease-loss

* BIG-PAR-103: tracker progress update

* fix(queue): harden shared-queue lease safety (BIG-PAR-103)

Summary:
- Serialize SQLite leasing with BEGIN IMMEDIATE to prevent double-lease races.
- Enforce lease ownership + attempt checks for file/memory queue operations.
- Reject requeue/dead-letter transitions once a task is cancelled/dead-lettered.
- Add a regression test covering concurrent LeaseNext across two SQLite clients.

Rationale:
- Multi-worker and multi-node runs share one SQLite queue file. Deferred
  transactions could allow multiple processes to select and lease the same
  task before either UPDATE executes.

Tests:
- cd bigclaw-go && go test ./...

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* BIG-PAR-103: purge cancelled leases after expiry

* BIG-PAR-103: tracker progress update (expiry purge)

* BIG-PAR-103: expose lease-loss metrics

commit.message:
fix(workflow): normalize branch and PR checks

Summary:
- strip worktree suffixes like `@<sha>` when deriving Symphony branch
  names from workspace directories
- replace scratch-file `.git` write probes with `git rev-parse`
  plus `test -w` guidance
- make PR lookup use the live current branch with one issue-key
  fallback before concluding no PR exists

Rationale:
- keep workflow automation aligned with the checked-out repository
  even when Linear metadata or workspace folder names drift
- avoid false blockers caused by brittle Git metadata probes and
  branch-name mismatches during PR discovery

Tests:
- not run (workflow/docs-only change)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
feat(feed): variant-aware GMC feed with item_group_id grouping (#805)

* feat(feed): add variant-aware GMC feed with item_group_id grouping

Emit one <item> per product variant for merchants with gmc_variants_enabled,
grouped by item_group_id. Maps color/storage to GMC variant attributes,
adds product_detail enrichment from key_specs, and preserves ?variant=
deep links through all storefront redirects.

- Add gmc_variants_enabled flag on merchants table (migration)
- Create variant-mapping utility (JSONB attrs → GMC color/size/product_detail)
- Extend feed builder with variant emission, dedup, and canonical_link
- Fetch variants via RPC + key_specs gated by merchant flag
- Propagate variant/spec query errors instead of silently swallowing
- XML-escape product_detail values to prevent malformed feeds
- Restore category-text fallback for legacy product URLs
- Preserve ?variant= in all 3 redirect paths (category, lowercase, products/)
- Add ?variant= preselection in both storefront templates (ogabassey + generic)

repository.name: Baci
repository.description: Experiment

commit.message:
fix flappy bird template: physics, collisions, scoring, and config merge

Rewrites the flappy bird template to fix multiple bugs:
- Deep merge config instead of shallow spread (nested defaults were lost)
- Use body.reset() for pipe collider position sync (bodies were stuck at 0,0)
- Circular bird hitbox for fairer collision detection
- Pipe recycling pool instead of unsafe forEach-destroy iteration
- Use collider (not overlap) for pipes/ground to prevent pass-through
- Phaser zones for score detection instead of hacky undefined sprite cast
- State machine enum replacing fragile boolean flags
- Proper shutdown() lifecycle instead of removeAllListeners()
- Delta-time bird rotation for framerate independence
- Dynamic gravity from config instead of hardcoded in Phaser setup

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(auth): clear cache and stores on logout (#807)

* fix(auth): clear query cache and stores on logout to prevent data leakage

Replaces closed PR #706.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(auth): use auth context signOut in onboarding step

Replace direct supabase.auth.signOut() call in step3-account with
useAuthSafe() from auth-context, ensuring browser storage (sessionStorage
and localStorage) is cleared on logout. Falls back to inline storage
clearing when AuthProvider is not mounted.

Resolves CodeRabbit critical finding on PR #807.

commit.message:
fix(well-known): credential sharing + admin intent filter cleanup (#804)

* chore: trigger CI

* fix(well-known): add credential sharing for admin app and remove www from intent filters

Add delegate_permission/common.get_login_creds to admin app's assetlinks.json
so Google Play Console can enable credential sharing for usebaci.com.

Remove www.usebaci.com from admin app's iOS associatedDomains and Android
intentFilters since Vercel 308-redirects www → bare domain, which causes
Android/Apple verification to fail.
